findings.message: ".env" (Environment variables file) is not in .gitignore — will be committed to git.
findings.fix: Add ".env" to your .gitignore file immediately. If you've already committed it, the file is in git history even after deletion. Run: "git rm --cached .env" then add to .gitignore. If pushed to a public repo, rotate ALL secrets in that file — they are compromised.
findings.prompt: SECURITY FIX: My .env file is not in .gitignore, which means my secrets will be committed to git. Add .env, .env.local, .env.production, .env.development, and .env.staging to .gitignore. Also add node_modules/ and .DS_Store if missing. If .env was already committed, run: git rm --cached .env && git commit -m "Remove .env from tracking". If it was pushed to a public repo, ROTATE ALL SECRETS immediately — they are compromised.
findings.platformNotes: Lovable/Base44/Canva Code: These platforms handle git internally — check platform settings for secret management. Replit: Use the Secrets tab instead of .env files.

findings.message: Empty catch block — errors silently swallowed
findings.fix: Return a generic error message to users ("Something went wrong"). Log full error details server-side only with console.error(). Never expose .stack or raw error objects in responses.
findings.prompt: SECURITY FIX: My error handling leaks internal details (stack traces, error messages, database info) to users. Find all catch blocks that send err.message or err.stack in responses. Replace with generic messages: catch (error) { console.error(error); return Response.json({ error: 'Something went wrong' }, { status: 500 }); }. Also find and fix any empty catch blocks — they silently swallow errors.
findings.platformNotes: All platforms: Always log full errors server-side (console.error) but return generic messages to users.

findings.message: Non-localhost HTTP URL — data sent unencrypted
findings.fix: Use HTTPS for all external connections. HTTP transmits data in plaintext — anyone on the network can read it.
findings.prompt: SECURITY FIX: I have insecure connection configurations. Find and fix: (1) HTTP URLs pointing to production servers — change to HTTPS. (2) rejectUnauthorized: false — remove it and fix the certificate issue instead. (3) CORS wildcard origin: "*" — replace with specific allowed domains. (4) cors() with no config — add { origin: process.env.CORS_ORIGIN, credentials: true } with your actual domain.
findings.platformNotes: All platforms: Always use HTTPS in production. Platform-hosted apps typically get HTTPS automatically.
